child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: sequence
child.description: Indicates the order in which the dosage instructions should be applied or interpreted.

child.name: text
child.description: Free text dosage instructions e.g. SIG.

child.name: additionalInstruction
child.description: Supplemental instructions to the patient on how to take the medication (e.g. "with meals" or"take half to one hour before food"...
child.longDesc: Supplemental instructions to the patient on how to take the medication (e.g. "with meals" or"take half to one hour before food") or warnings for the patient about the medication (e.g. "may cause drowsiness" or "avoid exposure of skin to direct sunlight or sunlamps").

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: patientInstruction
child.description: Instructions in terms that are understood by the patient or consumer.

child.name: timing
child.description: When medication should be administered.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: event
child.description: Identifies specific times when the event occurs.

child.name: repeat
child.description: A set of rules that describe when the event is scheduled.

savedDerivedCopy.name: repeat
savedDerivedCopy.description: A set of rules that describe when the event is scheduled.

child.name: code
child.description: A code for the timing schedule (or just text in code.text). Some codes such as BID are ubiquitous, but many institutions define ...
child.longDesc: A code for the timing schedule (or just text in code.text). Some codes such as BID are ubiquitous, but many institutions define their own additional codes. If a code is provided, the code is understood to be a complete statement of whatever is specified in the structured timing data, and either the code or the data may be used to interpret the Timing, with the exception that .repeat.bounds still applies over the code (and is not contained in the code).

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: asNeeded
child.description: Indicates whether the Medication is only taken when needed within a specific dosing schedule (Boolean option).

child.name: asNeededFor
child.description: Indicates whether the Medication is only taken based on a precondition for taking the Medication (CodeableConcept).

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: site
child.description: Body site to administer to.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: route
child.description: How drug should enter body.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: method
child.description: Technique for administering medication.

child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: doseAndRate
child.description: Depending on the resource,this is the amount of medication administered, to be administered or typical amount to be administere...
child.longDesc: Depending on the resource,this is the amount of medication administered, to be administered or typical amount to be administered.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: type
child.description: The kind of dose or rate specified, for example, ordered or calculated.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: doseRange
child.description: Amount of medication per dose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: low
child.description: The low limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: high
child.description: The high limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: doseQuantity
child.description: Amount of medication per dose.

child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: rateRatio
child.description: Amount of medication per unit of time.

child.description: The value of the numerator.

child.description: Upper limit on medication per administration.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: maxDosePerLifetime
child.description: Upper limit on medication per lifetime of the patient.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.
